llm_result.summary: This plan proposes modifiable bio-psychological-social levers tailored to chronic back pain with mood disturbance. The levers target (1) pain experience and physical functioning (BIO), (2) cognitive-affective responses to pain (PSYCHO), and (3) social-context supports (SOCIAL), including their interactions (BIOxPSYCHO, PSYCHOxSOCIAL, BIOxSOCIAL, and BIOxPSYCHOxSOCIAL). By reducing catastrophizing, improving sleep and coping self-efficacy, increasing adaptive activity, enhancing social connectedness, and enabling data-guided feedback, the approach aims to lower baseline pain interference, decrease flare-related distress, and raise optimism and mood during flares.

solutions.predictor_variable: CBT-based Pain Coping Skills plus Graded Activity (behavioral activation)
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement an 8–12 week integrated program combining pain coping skills training (cognitive restructuring of pain catastrophizing, reframing pain narratives) with a graded activity/pacing plan to safely increase functional activity.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Addresses catastrophic thinking (C03), mood during flares (C04), and perceived coping self-efficacy (C07) by teaching skills and gradually increasing activity to demonstrate that manageable progress is possible.
solutions.measurement: PCS scores (catastrophizing), mood ratings (0–10), daily activity logs (steps/time spent active), pain intensity (0–10).
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for overexertion during graded activity; adjust pace to flare tolerance. If worsening mood or thoughts of self-harm emerge, escalate to professional care.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S01 to C03 (catastrophizing), C04 (mood during flares), and C07 (coping self-efficacy).

solutions.predictor_variable: Sleep hygiene optimization (consistent schedule, wind-down routine, caffeine timing, light exposure)
solutions.solution_candidate: Provide a personalized sleep optimization plan with regular bed/wake times, pre-sleep routines, caffeine management, light exposure during day, and a cool-dark sleep environment; reassess after 6–8 weeks.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep quality and regularity can decrease pain sensitivity fluctuations, reduce negative mood during flares, and bolster optimistic outlook (C06) by stabilizing circadian-related mood regulation.
solutions.measurement: Sleep efficiency (diary or wearable), time in bed vs. time asleep, WASO (wake after sleep onset), 0–10 mood rating.
solutions.safety_notes: If insomnia worsens or daytime fatigue increases, reassess sleep plan and consult clinician.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S02 to C04 (mood during flares) and C06 (optimism/hope).

solutions.predictor_variable: Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) / ACT for pain distress
solutions.solution_candidate: Deliver an 8-week MBCT/ACT-based program focusing on nonjudgmental awareness of pain, cognitive defusion, and values-based action to reduce reactivity to pain flare-ups.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces cognitive reactivity to pain (C03), improves mood during flares (C04), sustains optimism via values-based action (C06), and enhances coping self-efficacy (C07) by fostering adaptable responses.
solutions.measurement: Pain catastrophizing (PCS), mood ratings, acceptance scales, engagement in values-driven activities.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for emotional distress during mindfulness practice; provide support resources if distress increases.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S03 to C03, C04, C06, and C07.

solutions.predictor_variable: Pain flare management with pacing, ergonomic adjustments, and gentle mobilization
solutions.solution_candidate: Educate and coach on pacing during flares, implement ergonomic workspace/posture adjustments, and prescribe a gentle mobilization plan to minimize flare severity.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Directly reduces baseline pain exposure (C01) and flare frequency/severity (C02), lowers distress (C04), reduces irritability from pain (C05), and supports coping self-efficacy (C07) through actionable strategies.
solutions.measurement: Baseline pain (0–10), flare days/week, peak flare intensity, irritability rating (0–10), mood.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure adjustments are ergonomic and safe; avoid aggressive exercises during high-pain periods.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S04 to C01, C02, C04, C05, and C07.

solutions.solution_candidate: A skills-based module to build confidence in managing pain in daily activities, using mastery experiences, problem-solving, and self-regulation strategies.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Strengthens perceived control over pain (C07), reduces mood downturn during flares (C04), and decreases catastrophic thinking by fostering proactive coping (C03).
solutions.measurement: PSEQ (pain self-efficacy), daily activity engagement, mood ratings.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for overgeneralization of control; ensure goals are realistic and pair with pacing.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S05 to C07, C04, and C03.

solutions.predictor_variable: Structured social support and community engagement (social prescribing)
solutions.solution_candidate: Facilitate connections to community resources, support groups, or buddy systems to reduce isolation and bolster mood and resilience.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social connectedness can buffer mood downturns (C04) and sustain optimistic outlook (C06) while enhancing coping resources (C07).
solutions.measurement: Social support scale, loneliness index, mood, pain interference.
solutions.safety_notes: Screen for safety in social contexts; ensure privacy and voluntary participation.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S06 to C04, C06, and C07.

solutions.solution_candidate: Adopt a whole-food anti-inflammatory dietary pattern (e.g., fruits/vegetables, fatty fish, fiber-rich foods) with adequate hydration and moderation of pro-inflammatory foods.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Dietary patterns can modulate systemic inflammation and mood states, potentially reducing pain-related distress and improving baseline mood (C04) and overall pain experience (C01).
solutions.measurement: Diet quality index, daily mood logs, pain intensity trends.
solutions.safety_notes: Consider allergies/intolerances and cultural dietary preferences; offer alternatives.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S07 to C04 and C01.

solutions.predictor_variable: Personalization monitoring and data feedback via wearables and self-assessment logs
solutions.solution_candidate: Set up a user-friendly dashboard aggregating pain, mood, sleep, activity, and coping logs with weekly feedback to adjust plans.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Frequent feedback and self-monitoring can help identify patterns, support timely coping, reduce catastrophizing, and optimize pacing.
solutions.measurement: Adherence to logging, correlations between pain/mood/sleep, user-reported outcomes, trend analyses.
solutions.safety_notes: Protect privacy; avoid over-reliance on data for clinical decisions without clinician input.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S08 to C01, C02, C04, and C07.
